task_completion.description: Navigate to the Administration > Lists management page
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot shows the OpenEMR interface with the 'Edit List' or 'Lists' page loaded, typically found under the Administration menu.

task_completion.description: Select the 'Referral Source' list for editing
task_completion.visual_evidence: The dropdown menu for selecting a list shows 'Referral Source' or 'refsource' selected, or the table header explicitly indicates the Referral Source list is currently being viewed.

task_completion.description: Enter the ID and Title for the new option
task_completion.visual_evidence: Input fields for a new list option are visible. The 'Title' field contains 'Westside Urgent Care'. The 'ID' field contains 'westside_uc' or a similar reasonable identifier.

task_completion.description: Verify the new option appears in the saved list
task_completion.visual_evidence: The list table displays a row with the Title 'Westside Urgent Care'. This confirms the addition was saved successfully.

task_completion.description: Ensure the new option is marked as Active
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the row for 'Westside Urgent Care', the 'Active' checkbox is checked or the status column indicates it is active.

integrity.description: Agent used the OpenEMR web interface
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a web browser displaying the OpenEMR application, not a terminal, database client, or code editor.
Screenshots show the navigation menus (Administration > Lists) being used or the page context implies standard navigation rather than direct URL injection.

integrity.description: The result is displayed within the application UI
integrity.visual_evidence: The final verification shows the option inside the OpenEMR list table styling, not just text output or a mocked confirmation.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The internal list_id for Referral Sources is 'refsource'. The expected Title for the new option is 'Westside Urgent Care'.
